feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Moving Legacy On-Premise Console Apps Scheduling & Monitoring to SAP CPI ?
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently evaluating the best architecture to integrate and monitor our legacy on-premise console applications (e.g., C# / Python scripts doing data processing and DB syncs).

 

Our Main Goal: We want proactive monitoring. If a job fails or a DB connection drops, IT should get an alert immediately, rather than waiting for business users to complain.

 

Currently, they are scheduled via Windows Task Scheduler. We are debating between two architectural approaches and would love your expert opinions:

 

Approach A (Initial Thought): Move Scheduling to SAP CPI

 

  • Wrap the legacy console apps into Web APIs.

  • Use SAP Cloud Connector to expose them.

  • Use SAP CPI (Cloud Integration) Timer to trigger these APIs every 10 minutes.

  • Reasoning: If the API fails, CPI will log an error, and we can monitor the iFlows.

 

Approach B (Proposed Alternative): Keep Local Scheduling & Push to SAP Cloud ALM

 

  • Keep using Windows Task Scheduler (No Cloud Connector needed).

  • Add a few lines of code (or a wrapper script) to the console apps to push a "Heartbeat" (Status: OK / ERROR) to SAP Cloud ALM (Integration & Exception Monitoring / Custom Metric) via REST API after execution.

  • Set a "Missing Event" rule in Cloud ALM. If the app doesn't send an 'OK' signal within 15 mins (e.g., server crash), Cloud ALM sends an alert.

 

My Questions for the Community:

 

  1. Is Approach A considered an anti-pattern? Will polling an on-premise system every 10 mins generate too many unnecessary Message Processing Logs (MPL) and increase costs/overhead in CPI?

  2. Is Approach B the recommended best practice for pure monitoring purposes?

  3. Has anyone implemented Approach B, and are there any blind spots I should be aware of?

Thank you !

entry.title: ELT practices with SAP
entry.content: We would like to explore SAP tools/standard practices for ELT processes to transfer data from SAAS applications to Snowflake.

entry.title: Use of Sequential Mapping Tab in TPM Agreement
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Could anyone please help me understand the purpose of the Sequential Mapping tab in the agreement section of TPM?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Looking to understand support challenges in SAP environments
entry.content:
I’m searching for support challenges in SAP environments

Interested in hearing real examples from support projects, warehouse operations, manufacturing integrations, or AMS environments.

entry.title: Usage of Data Store and its Impact
entry.content:
We have a requirement to send latest data once a day from MDG to Receiver and we have few approach to fulfill the same. We are looking for best solution/suggestion.

We have a requirement to send Material master data (latest) from MDG via DRF to Receiver daily once and we have below 3 approach

1) MDG is already sending data from MDG using DRF in real time to CPI, CPI is sending to AEM and using other iflow, CPI is picking data from AEM and sending to receiver.
But as our requirement is to send data in batches once a day, so in this case, MDG sends data in batches once a day to CPI via DRF and CPI sends to AEM, from AEM, CPI again picks data and send to receiver.
As we dont have picking data from AEM (based on some schedule), so we have come up with approach.

2) As MDG already sending data to CPI in real time, so we can create another queue, pick from AEM and then send data to Datastore (overwrite the records based on key field) and then using another iflows, with timer/scheduler, CPI can pick all data from data store and send to receiver.
3) Use the same approach as option 2 and instead of using Data store, we can use other external database like datalake, etc.

Can you please confirm which one can be best suited here ? and any pros and cons of using DataStore (where we have to store data for 24 hours) or any other recommendation in a case where CPI has to pick data from AEM and send in batches to receiver ?

Regards,
Saurav Kumar

entry.title: Download BTP Subaccount Audit log recods into CSV File daily
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I want to Create iFlow to extract auditlog records  daily for BTP Subaccount in CF.  iFlow should be triggered by program in  S/4HANA,  iFlow must call the /v2/auditlogrecords API with server-side paging for over 500 records, filter by event types (data-access, data-modification, security-events, configuration), aggregate results, convert to CSV, and enable download to local PC or email the file. JSON file format is not accepted in our organization.

Has anyone implemented a similar iFlow design? Any tips on handling multi-subaccount auth/paging or better alternatives?

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP CPI TPM - Issues with Custom Mappings in new Agreements
entry.content:
Hello all,

Since today I face an issue with SAP CPI TPM. I tried to set up a new agreement on our DEV system which is more or less a copy of what's already running in production mode.

While I have 51 String Parameters for this scenario in CPI PRD I see only 50 in CPI DEV. The missing parameter is the SAP_EDI_REC_Custom_Main_Mapping which holds the information on the CUSTOM_MAPPING I need in this scenario. I made sure to have the latest "Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2" and tried to create the agreement from scratch two times after I knew what's missing but this parameter is not being set.
The B2B Scenarios setup dialog did change  and I think that the logic behind this is currently broken. Does any of you face the same issue and/or do you have any idea how to solve or bypass this issue?

On SAP CPI DEV we are currently using these software versions

SAP Integration Suite
Version - 4.8.2
Tenant - ****
Service Plan - standard_edition
    
 
Cloud Integration
6.78.49
  
 
Cloud Integration Runtime
8.42.10
  
 
API Management
2.15.3
  
 
Integration Advisor
2.12.3
  

Kind regards,
Mathias

entry.title: in API managment, deleted policy but upon deploy, it is giving issue
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created a policy SetCORS in PostFlow and deployed it. After that, i deleted the policy from the 'Created Policies' tab first, before deleting from the flow.

Now, it is grayed out in Flow and i am not able to remove it. If i am trying to deploy it is giving error "Invalid policy reference SetCORS in endpoint default"

entry.title: 401 in Integration Suite - unable to access JMS
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

My integration Suite suddenly unable to show the JMS queue facility.  I already done relogging and clear cache, i also use incognito. I am not sure where to fix this.

entry.title: SAP CPI OAuth2 Client Credentials failing with 400 error — how to fix?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m trying to use OAuth2 Client Credentials authentication in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI), but I’m getting an 400 error when the iFlow tries to fetch the token. I’d appreciate guidance on what might be misconfigured and how to resolve it.

I'm attaching the screenshots of all the configurations.

1. OAuth2 Client Credentials configured in security materials.

I'm calling this in HTTP to fetch token.

I'm also passing Content-Type =  application/x-www-form-urlencoded in header in HTTP adapter.

2. I can see Authorization in Runlogs with Bearer ***

3. Getting 400 error asking for 'grant-_type' in response payload.

P.s : please correct me if I'm wrong, while using OAuth2 Client Credentials, by default "grant_type" is "client_credentials" and it goes in body.

entry.title: Integration Suite Access
entry.content: I am facing this while accesing the created integration suite in BTP. Also, integration provisioner role was assigned and relogged in to check if it is accessible, still no.

entry.title: CPI logic to ensure that two files are sent together in a single request.
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

In our project for Ariba Remittance, the requirement is to send two interdependent files together as a single bundle. Currently, CPI is sending these files one by one, which is not aligned with Ariba’s expectation. 

we require guidance specifically on the CPI logic to ensure that both files are sent together in a single request. 

Thanks in advance. 

Regards, VS

entry.title: Asset transfer within the co code depreciation issue
entry.content:
• Formwork has been purchased on multiple dates.
  • There have been several transfers made within the profit centres based on requirements
  • Depreciation is being calculated on a weighted average basis based on purchase date of each individual lot
  • Due to these transfers, the depreciation calculation is impacting individual projects depreciation expense.
  • However, the overall depreciation percentage remains consistent.
  • Kindly advise a suitable mechanism to  be carried out to rectify the depreciation discrepancies. Using the T code ABUMN

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Runtime APIs
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am currently working on leveraging SAP Integration Suite Runtime APIs and have been exploring available documentation and resources.

To ensure alignment with SAP-recommended approaches and avoid any deviations from best practices, I would like to request your guidance on the following areas:

  1. Official References
    • Links to authentic and up-to-date SAP documentation for Runtime APIs.
    • Any relevant API specifications or SAP Notes that cover this topic.
  2. Recommended Approach
    • Best practices for consuming Runtime APIs in enterprise scenarios.
    • Guidance on authentication strategies, error handling, and scalability considerations.
  3. Implementation Insights
    • Any sample use cases, integration patterns, or reference implementations.
    • Recommendations based on typical customer usage.
  4. Configuration Considerations
    • Key prerequisites, roles, and configuration steps to ensure correct setup.
    • Any known limitations or important considerations.
  5. Troubleshooting & Monitoring
    • Common challenges observed and suggested resolutions.
    • Recommended approach for logging, monitoring, and diagnostics.

I have already started reviewing the available materials but wanted to validate my approach against SAP's recommended standards and see if there are any additional resources I should refer to.

entry.title: Can a programmatically / AI-generated iFlow ZIP be uploaded into SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
**What I'm trying to do**
Generate a valid iFlow ZIP using AI (without manually designing in the editor) and upload it via *Add → Upload → Single File*.

**ZIP structure used:**
```
META-INF/MANIFEST.MF
src/main/resources/script/SalesOrderMapping.groovy
src/main/resources/scenarioflows/integrationflow/SF_to_S4HANA_SalesOrder.iflw
```
MANIFEST.MF includes `SAP-BundleType: IntegrationFlow`, single file, at ZIP root.

**Error:**
> Upload of the resource failed. Integration Flow project is invalid.

**Already checked:** single MANIFEST, correct root level, no __MACOSX folders, Bundle-SymbolicName matches artifact name.

**Questions:**
1. Is this approach feasible at all?
2. Are there undocumented required files (e.g. `parameter.prop`)?
3. Does `.iflw` need a `bpmndi:BPMNDiagram` layout section to be valid?

Happy to share the full files. Any working minimal ZIP example would be very helpful.

entry.title: SAP CI Connection to Salesforce fails intermittently - works after iFlow redeployment
entry.content:
Hello All,

I am working on an interface in SAP Cloud Integration which involves multiple iFlows connected by ProcessDirect adapter. These iFlows call Salesforce using the Salesforce adapter with Authentication set as "OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer".

The calls to Salesforce works fine except that they sometimes fail with the following error:

Error: org.apache.camel.CamelException: [{"message":"INVALID_JWT_FORMAT","errorCode":"INVALID_AUTH_HEADER"}]

This happens especially when the iFlow is called from another iFlow using the ProcessDirect adapter. even though both the iFlows have calls to Salesforce using the same authentication method, the calls (intermittently) fails in the consumer iFlow with above error.

Also, it starts working after we deploy the consumer iFlow again. 

I can confirm that the Authentication credentials being used are correct as they also work most of the times. This seems to be some runtime cache related issue which gets resolved after the iFlow is deployed again. Please suggest how could this issue be resolved?

Regards,

Faisal

entry.title: France E-Invoicing Set up from S4H public Cloud Via SAP DRC
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

We need to implement the France E-Invoicing Process from the SAP S4H public cloud via approved platform which is SAP DRC cloud edition. 

We are using the SAP BTP subaccount which is under the region cf-eu10. But the below SAP help document mentioned that  in order to use the Process for the France , cf-eu02 subaccount is required.

Does that mean in Order to use the France E-invoice process cf-eu02 subaccount is mandatory or do we any alternative process that can be used within the cf-eu10?


Also we didn't understand the below note  given the SAP document . Attached the screenshot as well.

Currently you can only connect with the qualification system of the tax authority. For that, you use the test environment of the cloud edition 


SAP help document: Supported Compliance Tasks | SAP Help Portal 




It's on Priority . Please check and confirm 

Regards

Jyothi

entry.title: Unable to see the calls in CPI Monitoring page
entry.content:
Hi Team,
We have 1 flow in CPI which has successfully developed. Flow is APIM- SAP CPI- HR system. We were able to test the flow and we were able to see the calls in HR back end system also. But In CPI monitoring page, we were unable to see the calls. We were able to see the Http logs also. Attached screenshot.

entry.title: Costs related to digital access resulting from integrations with non-SAP applications
entry.content: If I have third-party systems that I want to integrate with SAP S/4HANA Private Edition 2023, I know there are costs associated with Digital Access per document created. Can I save these costs by using an intermediate SAP HANA Cloud table in BTP for synchronous integrations, and for asynchronous integrations, using SFTP via unattended bots? Or could SAP still charge me for Digital Access during the audit?

entry.title: Cloud Integration metering for outbound payloads >250KB does not increase Total Messages
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are performing controlled transaction metering tests in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) and we found a behavior that seems inconsistent with the official documentation regarding payloads larger than 250KB.

Scenario

Integration Flow:

HTTPS Sender → XML to JSON Transformation → HTTP Receiver Adapter → External Node.js API

Additionally, the iFlow performs another outbound HTTP call to an external monitoring API.

The flow does NOT contain:

  • Splitter
  • Multicast
  • JMS
  • DataStore
  • Retries

Test Execution

We executed the test using Postman Runner with:

  • 345 iterations

  • Inbound XML payload size ≈ 690 KB

  • Outbound JSON payload size after transformation ≈ 463 KB

We also verified the following exchange properties:

SAP_InboundMessageSizeInKB = 690
content-length ≈ 689558

Additionally, we logged the outbound payload before the HTTP receiver adapter:

OutboundSizeKB ≈ 463

Expected Behavior

According to SAP documentation:

“The combined (aggregated) size of all outbound transactions via one or more receiver(s) adapter is considered for transaction metering.”

“If it is more than 250KB, it will be counted in blocks of 250KB.”

Based on this rule, we expected:

463KB / 250KB ≈ 2 transactions per execution

Therefore:

345 executions ≈ 690 Total Messages

Observed Behavior

The Message Usage Dashboard shows:

  • MPL Count = 345

  • Total Messages = 345

Meaning:

  • each execution is counted as only 1 message

  • payload size >250KB does not appear to increase the Total Messages counter

Additional Observation

When testing integration flows with Splitter patterns, the Message Usage Dashboard DOES increase Total Messages correctly according to the number of split outbound transactions.

This suggests that:

  • Splitter-based outbound transactions are correctly metered

  • but outbound HTTP payload size >250KB in a simple HTTP receiver scenario may not be metered as expected

Question

Can someone clarify:

  1. Is transaction metering in Cloud Integration really based on outbound payload size blocks of 250KB for HTTP receiver adapters?

  2. Does the Message Usage Dashboard aggregate or simplify payload-based metering?

  3. Is this behavior different in BTP Trial vs productive tenants?

  4. Is the 250KB rule applied only under specific integration patterns?

Any clarification from SAP product experts or architects would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks.

Evidence:

Message Usage Dashboard

 

SAP Inbound & Outbound Size KB

 

iFlow Diagram

 

Splitter Comparison
